message:
chore(ft-xbnl0.2.3): silence dead-code warning on legacy capture_from_provider (tick 306)

Adds `#[allow(dead_code)]` to `SnapshotEngine::capture_from_provider`
(snapshot_engine.rs L769) with a doc comment explaining why it's
retained:

- Legacy ambient path preserved alongside the cx-first sibling
  `capture_from_provider_with_cx` (tick 111/112 scaffolding)
- The current periodic scheduler body (tick 296 cx-first migration)
  uses the `_with_cx` sibling; the ambient version is kept for any
  future ambient caller under the legacy-path-preserved contract.

Reduces baseline warning count 4 → 3.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 120 (187-306).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 68 (unchanged).
main.rs cross-crate total: 236 sites (unchanged).

Grand total: 304 cx-first migrations (unchanged; warning cleanup only).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -766,6 +766,11 @@ impl SnapshotEngine {
 
     /// Attempt a capture via the pane provider, with standard logging.
     /// Returns `true` if a new checkpoint was persisted.
+    ///
+    /// Retained as a legacy ambient path alongside the cx-first sibling
+    /// `capture_from_provider_with_cx` used by `run_periodic_with_cx`
+    /// and the current periodic scheduler (ticks 111/112).
+    #[allow(dead_code)]
     async fn capture_from_provider<F, Fut>(
         &self,
         pane_provider: &F,
